options: True — a short-lived service token obtained via POST to the auth/token endpoint is passed as X-Auth-Token on all subsequent API calls
False — DNA Center uses Basic Authentication on every request; no token exchange is required

questions.q: A DNA Center OBSERVER-ROLE token that successfully calls the global-credential endpoint indicates which security condition?
options: Normal behavior — OBSERVER tokens are designed to read all device credentials for monitoring purposes
An insufficient authorization check vulnerability — global credentials should require SUPER-ADMIN role and the OBSERVER token should receive a 403 Forbidden response

questions.q:
Long-lived DNA Center integration tokens should be rotated regularly and invalidated immediately after any credential exposure event.
True or False?
options: True — long-lived tokens (valid up to 6 months) represent persistent access risk; immediate invalidation limits the exposure window after a compromise
False — long-lived tokens are designed for stability; rotating them frequently causes automation outages and is not a security best practice

questions.q:
A WINWORD.EXE process spawning powershell.exe with an -EncodedCommand argument is a high-confidence indicator of malicious macro execution.
True or False?
options: True — Office applications spawning PowerShell with encoded commands is a known-bad pattern mapped to ATT&CK T1059.001 and is almost exclusively associated with malicious macros
False — encoded PowerShell commands from Office applications are commonly used by legitimate IT automation tools and have a high false positive rate

questions.q: In a phishing-to-domain-compromise kill chain, what MITRE ATT&CK technique does NTLM authentication from a workstation to a domain controller represent?
options: T1566.001 — Spearphishing Attachment (initial access via email)
T1550.002 — Pass the Hash (lateral movement using stolen NTLM credential material without knowing the plaintext password)

questions.q:
Cisco Umbrella detecting a DNS query to a newly-registered domain 45 seconds after PowerShell execution is a significant kill chain correlation signal.
True or False?
options: True — the temporal relationship between PowerShell execution and a newly-registered domain DNS query indicates C2 callback (ATT&CK T1071.001)
False — newly-registered domains are queried frequently in normal corporate browsing; the timing correlation is coincidental without additional evidence

questions.q: Which combination of data sources provides the highest-confidence detection of the phishing-to-domain-compromise kill chain?
options: Email gateway alone — detecting the malicious attachment before delivery prevents all downstream kill chain stages
Correlated telemetry from email gateway (attachment verdict), Secure Endpoint (process chain), and Umbrella (C2 DNS) — no single source sees the complete picture

questions.q:
XDR automated response playbooks can quarantine a compromised host within seconds of analyst approval, compared to manual containment that may take 30-60 minutes.
True or False?
options: True — XDR sends quarantine commands to Cisco Secure Endpoint via API, removing the host from the network in seconds once the playbook is triggered
False — endpoint quarantine always requires a direct connection to the endpoint and takes several minutes regardless of the management platform used

questions.q:
Cisco Silicon One's MACsec implementation operates at line rate (25.6 Tbps) without any throughput penalty on packet forwarding.
True or False?
options: True — MACsec GCM-AES-256 is hardware-accelerated in the Silicon One ASIC and does not impact forwarding throughput
False — MACsec encryption adds computational overhead that reduces throughput by approximately 15-20% on Silicon One

questions.q: An unauthorized gRPC telemetry subscriber on a Silicon One router can receive what type of sensitive operational data?
options: Only aggregate interface byte counts with a 5-minute polling interval — no real-time or per-flow data is exposed
Per-second interface utilization, per-flow statistics by destination AS, and forwarding table statistics — revealing the complete network traffic matrix

questions.q:
Mutual TLS (mTLS) on gRPC telemetry requires both a server certificate and a valid client certificate before a subscription is accepted.
True or False?
options: True — mTLS enforces bidirectional certificate authentication, ensuring only authorized collectors can subscribe to Silicon One telemetry streams
False — mTLS only validates the server certificate; client authentication on gRPC telemetry requires a separate API key mechanism

questions.q:
A compromised IOS XR control plane can reprogram Silicon One's P4 forwarding tables to redirect or drop traffic at wire speed.
True or False?
options: True — the control plane programs Silicon One's match-action tables via P4Runtime API; a compromised control plane has write access to all forwarding rules enforced at 25.6 Tbps
False — Silicon One's hardware includes a cryptographic lock that prevents the control plane from modifying forwarding tables without physical access

questions.q: MACsec on Silicon One provides protection against which specific threat on inter-datacenter links?
options: Denial-of-service attacks targeting the data plane — MACsec rate-limits incoming frames to prevent flooding
Eavesdropping and replay attacks on Ethernet frame payloads — MACsec encrypts the frame payload and authenticates the frame header at each hop

questions.q:
The HARVEST NOW, DECRYPT LATER (HNDL) threat requires adversaries to have a quantum computer before they begin collecting encrypted traffic.
True or False?
options: True — without a quantum computer, collection has no value; nation-states wait until decryption is feasible before archiving
False — HNDL posture involves collecting and archiving encrypted traffic today with the intention of decrypting it retrospectively once quantum computers become available

questions.q:
IKEv2 hybrid PQC (RFC 9370) combines classical ECDH-P256 with ML-KEM-768 so that the session key is secure against both classical and quantum attacks.
True or False?
options: True — the hybrid approach derives the session key from both shared secrets; breaking either algorithm alone is insufficient to recover the key
False — hybrid PQC uses ML-KEM-768 exclusively and deprecates ECDH-P256, which is removed from the IKEv2 proposal to prevent downgrade

questions.q:
Cisco IOS XR 7.9+ supports IKEv2 hybrid PQC with graceful fallback for peers that do not support ML-KEM.
True or False?
options: True — if the remote peer does not support ML-KEM-768, the IKEv2 exchange falls back to classical ECDH-P256, enabling phased deployment
False — enabling the PQC-HYBRID proposal breaks compatibility with all classical-only IKEv2 peers; both endpoints must upgrade simultaneously

questions.q: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) and Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) both rely on computational hardness assumptions for their security guarantees.
options: False — QKD uses quantum physics laws for information-theoretic security (not computational hardness), while PQC relies on mathematical problems believed to be hard for quantum computers
True — both QKD and PQC depend on the difficulty of lattice-based mathematical problems standardized by NIST

questions.q:
MACsec GCM-AES-256 on Silicon One provides adequate near-term quantum resistance because Grover's algorithm only halves the effective key length, leaving 128 bits of quantum security.
True or False?
options: True — 256-bit symmetric keys reduced to 128-bit effective security by Grover's algorithm still provide an acceptable security margin for current threat timelines
False — Grover's algorithm breaks GCM-AES-256 completely; symmetric encryption provides no quantum resistance without post-quantum cipher suite upgrades

questions.q: True or False: HTTP encrypts data using TLS before sending it across the network.
options: True — HTTP uses TLS encryption
False — HTTP transmits data in plaintext

questions.q: The Firesheep exploit worked because Facebook used HTTP for browsing after logging in. What could an attacker steal to hijack another user's account?
options: The user's password from the login page
The user's unencrypted session cookie from ongoing HTTP traffic

questions.q: True or False: A login page that uses HTTPS guarantees your session stays secure even if the rest of the site uses HTTP.
options: False — a plaintext session cookie can be stolen to hijack your account after login
True — HTTPS on the login step protects the entire session

questions.q: Which part of the TLS handshake proves the server is who it claims to be?
options: The server sends its certificate, which the browser verifies against trusted Certificate Authorities
The server and browser agree on a symmetric AES-256 key using Diffie-Hellman

questions.q: What does a 'Not Secure' warning in your browser's address bar indicate?
options: The site's server is temporarily overloaded and requests may be slow
The page is using HTTP or has an invalid TLS certificate, meaning traffic is not encrypted

questions.q: True or False: Browser DevTools (F12) can only show HTML source code — they cannot reveal cookies or outgoing network requests.
options: True — DevTools is limited to viewing page source only
False — DevTools shows every request, response, cookie, and script the browser processes

questions.q: The British Airways Magecart attack injected 22 lines of JavaScript into the checkout page. Why couldn't customers visually detect the attack?
options: The injected script ran in the browser exactly like legitimate JavaScript — it was invisible to the user
The script disabled all browser security warnings before executing

questions.q: What is the browser's Same-Origin Policy (SOP) designed to prevent?
options: JavaScript on one page from reading data from a page on a different origin
Users from accessing HTTP pages when an HTTPS version is available

questions.q: True or False: HTML comments in page source are hidden from regular users and cannot be read without special tools.
options: True — HTML comments are stripped before the browser renders the page
False — anyone can view HTML comments using View Page Source or browser DevTools

questions.q: A Content Security Policy (CSP) header addresses which browser security problem?
options: It restricts which script sources a browser will execute, blocking unauthorized injected scripts
It forces all cookies to use the Secure flag so they are only sent over HTTPS

questions.q: In an IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability, what does the server fail to do?
options: Verify that the requesting client is authorized to access the object ID it sent
Encrypt the response body before sending it back to the client

questions.q: True or False: In the client-server model, a client can become the server within the same HTTP request-response cycle.
options: False — in a given request-response exchange, the roles of client and server are fixed
True — modern HTTP/2 allows the client to push responses back to the server

questions.q: Which HTTP method is used to retrieve data from a server without modifying it?
options: POST — it sends a body with the request to retrieve filtered results
GET — it retrieves data and does not modify server state

questions.q: The Facebook Business Manager API IDOR in 2019 exposed users' phone numbers. What was the root cause?
options: The API returned any user's phone number given their user ID without checking if the caller was authorized to see it
An attacker cracked Facebook's encryption by exploiting a weak cipher in the TLS handshake

questions.q: True or False: A server can safely trust object IDs sent by a client as long as those IDs are numeric.
options: True — numeric IDs are harder to guess, so they are safe to use without authorization checks
False — the server must always verify whether the requester is authorized to access the object, regardless of ID format

questions.q: True or False: A TLS certificate with a valid signature from Let's Encrypt guarantees the website is legitimate and trustworthy.
options: True — Let's Encrypt only issues certificates to verified, trustworthy organizations
False — a Let's Encrypt certificate only proves domain control; phishing sites can obtain them too

questions.q: The DigiNotar hack allowed attackers to intercept Gmail for thousands of Iranian users. How was this possible?
options: Attackers used DigiNotar's compromised CA to issue fraudulent Google certificates accepted as valid by browsers
Attackers exploited a TLS protocol flaw that allowed them to decrypt AES-256 session keys

questions.q: When a browser encounters a certificate where the Common Name (CN) does not match the domain you are visiting, what does this indicate?
options: The server is using an older TLS version and needs to be upgraded to TLS 1.3
The certificate may have been reused from a different site, which is a potential man-in-the-middle indicator

questions.q: True or False: Clicking through a browser's 'Your connection is not private' warning is acceptable if you recognize the website.
options: True — the warning is often a false positive caused by expired certificates on legitimate sites
False — the warning means the server's identity is unverified; you may be communicating with an impersonator

questions.q: What was the purpose of Certificate Transparency logs, introduced after the DigiNotar incident?
options: To make all certificate issuance publicly auditable so unauthorized certificates can be detected
To allow browsers to cache certificates locally and reduce the number of CA verification requests

questions.q: True or False: The HttpOnly cookie attribute prevents JavaScript from reading the cookie via document.cookie.
options: True — HttpOnly blocks JavaScript access, so XSS attacks cannot steal the cookie
False — HttpOnly only prevents the cookie from being sent over HTTP; JavaScript can still read it

questions.q: Why does stealing a session cookie give an attacker full account access without knowing the user's password?
options: Session tokens are derived from the password, so possessing the token is equivalent to knowing the password
The server identifies the user solely by the session token; a valid token grants full access regardless of how it was obtained

questions.q: True or False: The SameSite=Strict cookie attribute is designed to protect against Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks.
options: True — SameSite=Strict prevents the cookie from being sent in cross-origin requests
False — SameSite is only a performance hint and has no effect on cross-site requests

questions.q: The 2022 Slack breach involved stolen session cookies. Which security mechanism did this attack bypass entirely?
options: Two-factor authentication, because the session was already authenticated when the cookie was issued
TLS encryption, because the attacker used a man-in-the-middle attack on an HTTP connection

questions.q: What is session fixation, and how is it prevented?
options: Session fixation is when an attacker sets a known session token before login; prevented by regenerating the session token on login
Session fixation is when a cookie expires prematurely; prevented by extending the session lifetime on each request

questions.q: True or False: API authentication tokens should be placed in the URL query string for convenience since URLs are encrypted by HTTPS.
options: True — HTTPS encrypts the URL so placing tokens in query strings is safe
False — URL parameters appear in server logs, browser history, and referrer headers, exposing the token

questions.q: The Peloton API breach exposed private data for 4.5 million users despite the Peloton app displaying limited information. What was the root vulnerability?
options: The API returned the full user object with all private fields to any caller, even without authentication
Peloton stored passwords in plaintext, allowing attackers to log in as any user

questions.q: True or False: An API that requires authentication on its public endpoints is automatically safe from excessive data exposure vulnerabilities.
options: False — even authenticated APIs can return more data than the app displays, giving direct callers access to hidden fields
True — authentication ensures users only see the data they are authorized to access

questions.q: What does 'rate limiting' protect against in the context of API security?
options: It prevents attackers from enumerating all records by limiting how many requests a caller can make in a time window
It prevents attackers from intercepting API responses by throttling unencrypted traffic

questions.q: According to the OWASP API Security Top 10, what is the most common API vulnerability?
options: Broken Object Level Authorization — server returns data for any object ID without checking if the requester owns it
SQL Injection — attackers embed malicious queries in the JSON request body to extract database records

questions.q: True or False: Bandwidth and throughput are the same measurement — both describe the actual data rate achieved on a network connection.
options: False — bandwidth is the maximum capacity; throughput is the actual data rate achieved, which is always at or below bandwidth
True — bandwidth and throughput are interchangeable terms for measuring network speed

questions.q: If a server has a 10 Gbps internal link but only a 1 Gbps ISP uplink, what is the effective maximum bandwidth for internet traffic?
options: 10 Gbps, because the internal link is the primary path for all data
1 Gbps, because the ISP uplink is the slowest link in the chain and becomes the bottleneck

questions.q: True or False: Latency and bandwidth measure the same thing — a high-bandwidth connection always has low latency.
options: True — more bandwidth means data arrives faster, which reduces delay
False — bandwidth is pipe size; latency is delay time. Satellite internet can have high bandwidth but very high latency

questions.q: The 2023 Cloudflare DDoS peaked at 71 million requests per second from 30,000 IPs. What made Cloudflare's architecture resilient against this bandwidth attack?
options: Cloudflare filtered all traffic through a single hardened data center equipped with specialized DDoS hardware
Cloudflare's distributed architecture across 285+ data centers spread the load so no single location was overwhelmed

questions.q: A DDoS bandwidth saturation attack aims to achieve what outcome?
options: Flood the target's network connection with more traffic than it can handle, so legitimate traffic is dropped
Exploit a software vulnerability in the target's web server to crash the application process

questions.q: True or False: A ping result of 20ms means data takes 20ms to travel one way from sender to receiver.
options: False — ping measures round-trip time (RTT); 20ms RTT means approximately 10ms each direction
True — ping measures one-way latency from the sender to the remote host

questions.q: Which tool shows per-hop latency along the full network path to a destination, helping identify where congestion occurs?
options: traceroute — it shows the round-trip time to each intermediate router along the path
iperf3 — it measures maximum throughput between two hosts on the same network

questions.q: True or False: High latency on a connection always means there is a bandwidth problem that needs to be fixed by upgrading the link speed.
options: True — high latency is always caused by insufficient bandwidth and requires a faster link
False — latency can be caused by physical distance, processing delays, or congestion — not necessarily insufficient bandwidth

questions.q: The 2012 AWS US-East-1 outage showed how latency causes cascading failures. What is the core mechanism of a latency cascade?
options: One slow component causes upstream callers to queue waiting for it, backing up the entire call chain until the system halts
High latency causes packet loss, which triggers TCP retransmissions that permanently corrupt the session state

questions.q: Why does CDN (Content Delivery Network) usage reduce latency for end users?
options: CDNs compress all data before transmission, reducing the number of bytes that must travel across the network
CDNs serve content from nodes geographically close to the user, reducing the physical distance data must travel

questions.q: True or False: DNS cache poisoning corrupts a resolver's cache so that users are redirected to an attacker-controlled IP instead of the legitimate server.
options: True — the poisoned cache entry causes all users on that resolver to reach the attacker's server
False — cache poisoning only affects the speed of DNS lookups, not the IP addresses returned

questions.q: The 2008 Kaminsky attack exploited a weakness in DNS to make cache poisoning practical. What was the fundamental flaw?
options: The 16-bit DNS transaction ID provided too little entropy, allowing attackers to guess the right ID and inject a fake response before the real one arrived
DNS responses were transmitted without TLS encryption, allowing any router to read and modify them in transit

questions.q: True or False: DNSSEC prevents cache poisoning by encrypting DNS traffic so it cannot be intercepted in transit.
options: False — DNSSEC adds cryptographic signatures to DNS records so tampering is detectable, but it does not encrypt DNS traffic
True — DNSSEC uses AES-256 encryption to protect DNS responses from interception and modification

questions.q: If a DNS resolver's cache shows 185.220.101.50 for yourbank.com but the authoritative nameserver returns 198.51.100.77, what does this discrepancy indicate?
options: The authoritative server is misconfigured and has the wrong IP, so the cached version is correct
The resolver's cache is poisoned — the cached IP is an attacker-controlled address, not the legitimate server

questions.q: What is a practical advantage of DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) compared to standard DNS for end users?
options: DoH prevents ISP interception and makes it harder for intermediaries to inject fake DNS responses
DoH eliminates the need for DNSSEC signatures because HTTPS already authenticates the DNS server

questions.q: True or False: The 'least connections' load balancing algorithm always routes each request to the next server in a fixed rotation, regardless of server load.
options: False — that describes round-robin; least connections routes to the server with the fewest active connections
True — least connections uses a rotation similar to round-robin but tracks connection counts

questions.q: A load balancer removes a backend server from the pool when the server fails its health check. What benefit does this provide?
options: It enables zero-downtime deployments by automatically removing unhealthy servers without manual intervention
It permanently blacklists the server IP so attackers cannot target it directly

questions.q: True or False: Placing an admin backend server in the same load balancer pool as public content servers is a security best practice because the load balancer controls who can reach it.
options: False — the load balancer routes traffic equally to all pool members, exposing admin backends to any traffic the LB receives
True — the load balancer acts as a security boundary that blocks unauthorized access to admin backends

questions.q: The 2022 Heroku breach demonstrated a risk specific to load balancers. What privileged capability made the load balancer a high-value target?
options: Load balancers have privileged network access to all backend servers in their pool, which attackers can exploit to reach internal services
Load balancers store TLS private keys in memory, allowing attackers to decrypt all traffic passing through them

questions.q: Which load balancing algorithm ensures the same client always reaches the same backend server on every request?
options: IP hash — the client's IP address is hashed to consistently map it to a specific backend server
Round-robin — it cycles through all servers in order, which naturally returns some clients to the same server over time

questions.q: The 2016 DNC hack began with a spear phishing email sent to which individual?
options: John Podesta, Hillary Clinton's campaign chairman
Hillary Clinton, the presidential candidate

questions.q: True or False: The display name in an email is a reliable way to verify who actually sent it.
options: True — email providers verify display names
False — the real sender is shown in the actual email domain, not the display name

questions.q: What is spear phishing?
options: A mass phishing campaign sent to millions of random recipients
A targeted phishing attack using personal details to appear credible to a specific victim

questions.q: Which email authentication failure is the strongest indicator that a message was not sent by the claimed domain?
options: DKIM, SPF, and DMARC all failing
The subject line containing urgent language

questions.q: True or False: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is the most financially damaging cybercrime category, costing $2.7 billion per year.
options: True
False — ransomware causes more financial damage than BEC

questions.q: True or False: Accounts protected by two-factor authentication are 99.9% less likely to be compromised than accounts without it.
options: True
False — the protection benefit is closer to 50%

questions.q: Why is SMS-based 2FA considered weaker than an authenticator app?
options: SMS codes expire faster than TOTP codes, making them harder to use
SMS codes can be intercepted via SIM swapping, while authenticator apps are immune to this attack

questions.q: How did the attacker compromise Uber's systems in the 2022 breach?
options: By exploiting a software vulnerability in Uber's VPN server
By spamming push notification approvals until a contractor clicked Approve out of frustration

questions.q: True or False: TOTP codes generated by authenticator apps work by communicating with the server in real time to receive each code.
options: True — codes are sent from the server to the app
False — both the server and app independently compute the same code using a shared secret and the current time

questions.q: What attack is number-matching 2FA specifically designed to defeat?
options: MFA fatigue attacks, where attackers spam push notifications hoping for an accidental approval
SIM swapping attacks that redirect SMS codes to the attacker's phone

questions.q: True or False: A long passphrase made of four random words is generally stronger than an 8-character password with complex substitutions like P@ssw0rd.
options: True — length increases entropy exponentially and substitutions are in attackers' dictionaries
False — complex character substitutions make shorter passwords more resistant to cracking

questions.q: What was the most significant outcome of the 2009 RockYou breach?
options: It proved that bcrypt hashing makes passwords uncrackable at scale
32 million passwords stored in plaintext were exposed, creating rockyou.txt — the most used password cracking wordlist

questions.q: At 100 billion guesses per second, approximately how long would it take to crack a password with 71 bits of entropy?
options: A few seconds to a few minutes
Approximately 750 years

questions.q: True or False: Salting a password hash prevents rainbow table attacks by ensuring two users with the same password have different stored hashes.
options: True
False — salting only slows down brute-force attacks, not rainbow table lookups

questions.q: Which hashing algorithm is most appropriate for storing user passwords in a modern application?
options: MD5 or SHA-1, because they are fast and widely supported
bcrypt or Argon2id, because they are deliberately slow and resistant to GPU-based cracking

questions.q: True or False: On an open WiFi network, an attacker must send packets to your device to capture your traffic.
options: True — active injection is required to intercept traffic
False — passive listening captures all unencrypted frames without sending a single packet

questions.q: What protection does HTTPS provide on a public WiFi network?
options: It encrypts the content of web traffic, though the destination domain remains visible via SNI
It fully encrypts all traffic including DNS queries and hides the destination domain entirely

questions.q: How did the Darkhotel APT group compromise executives on hotel WiFi networks?
options: By stealing hotel WiFi passwords using brute-force attacks against the router
By sitting on the hotel network as a man-in-the-middle and injecting malicious fake software update prompts into HTTP connections

questions.q: True or False: On WPA2 personal WiFi, if you know the network password you can decrypt other users' traffic.
options: True — all devices on a WPA2 personal network share the same encryption key
False — WPA2 uses per-session keys that prevent other users from decrypting your traffic

questions.q: Which option provides the strongest protection when you must use an untrusted network?
options: Using HTTPS-only websites, which encrypts content and hides all metadata
Using your phone's mobile hotspot, which creates an isolated 4G/5G channel away from shared WiFi

questions.q: True or False: Social engineering attacks require exploiting a software vulnerability to succeed.
options: True — attackers need a technical exploit entry point
False — social engineering manipulates people directly, bypassing all technical controls

questions.q: In the Facebook/Google BEC scam, how did attacker Evaldas Rimasauskas steal over $120 million?
options: By hacking into the companies' financial systems using malware
By sending fake invoices impersonating a real supplier that both companies regularly paid

questions.q: Which psychological lever does urgency exploit in social engineering attacks?
options: It overrides the victim's rational decision-making by creating panic and time pressure
It makes the victim feel they are being watched and must comply to avoid punishment

questions.q: True or False: Legitimate IT support staff will sometimes need your password to help diagnose and fix account issues.
options: True — IT teams require credentials to access accounts for troubleshooting
False — legitimate IT never needs your password; a request for it is a red flag

questions.q: What is the correct way to verify the identity of someone claiming to be from your IT department or a security agency?
options: Ask them for their employee ID number and write it down for your records
Hang up and call back using a known official number, not the one they provided

questions.q: True or False: Most real-world cyberattacks exploit zero-day vulnerabilities that have no available patch.
options: True — attackers primarily rely on unknown vulnerabilities
False — most attacks exploit known, patchable vulnerabilities because most systems are poorly patched

questions.q: The WannaCry ransomware infected 200,000 computers in 2017. What was the key factor that made this possible?
options: The attackers discovered a novel zero-day that no one knew about
A patch for the exploited vulnerability (MS17-010) had been available for two months but was not applied

questions.q: What does a CVSS score of 9.8 indicate about a vulnerability?
options: It is a critical severity vulnerability, likely allowing remote code execution with no authentication
It is a moderate severity vulnerability that requires local access to exploit

questions.q: True or False: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) required attackers to have prior access to a system before they could exploit it.
options: True — Log4Shell required authenticated access to trigger
False — sending a single specially crafted string that got logged was enough to trigger remote code execution

questions.q: What is a zero-day vulnerability?
options: A vulnerability unknown to the vendor for which no patch yet exists
A vulnerability that was patched on the same day it was publicly disclosed

questions.q: True or False: A data breach only puts you at risk if you made a security mistake with that company.
options: True — breaches only expose users who had weak passwords
False — your data is exposed whenever the company holding it is breached, regardless of your own security practices

questions.q: What is credential stuffing?
options: An attack where stolen username and password pairs from one breach are tested automatically against other sites
A technique for guessing passwords by trying every possible combination of characters

questions.q: How long did Yahoo's 2013 breach of 3 billion accounts go undetected before disclosure?
options: Approximately three years, discovered in 2016
Approximately six months, discovered in early 2014

questions.q: True or False: The Have I Been Pwned API uses k-anonymity, meaning your actual password is never transmitted when checking if it has been exposed.
options: True — only the first 5 characters of the SHA-1 hash are sent to the API
False — the full password hash is sent to the server for comparison

questions.q: Why are security question answers particularly dangerous when exposed in a data breach?
options: Security questions are harder to change than passwords, and the answers often remain valid across accounts indefinitely
Security question answers are stored in plaintext, making them easier to crack than hashed passwords

questions.q: Given the URL https://monterey-aquarium.org.phish-site.ru/tickets — what is the actual domain?
options: monterey-aquarium.org, since that appears first in the URL
phish-site.ru, because the real domain is always the rightmost portion before the first slash

questions.q: True or False: An HTTPS padlock in the browser means a website is safe and not a phishing site.
options: True — HTTPS proves the site is legitimate
False — HTTPS only proves the connection is encrypted; phishing sites routinely use HTTPS with valid certificates

questions.q: In Operation Aurora, how did attackers compromise Google employees' devices?
options: By sending email attachments that required the victim to manually download and run a file
By directing targets to a malicious website that exploited an Internet Explorer zero-day via a drive-by download requiring no additional clicks

questions.q: True or False: URL shorteners like bit.ly always reveal their final destination in the link preview before you click.
options: True — all major URL shorteners display the destination URL on hover
False — URL shorteners hide the real destination; you should expand them using tools like curl or unshorten.me before clicking

questions.q: Which method is most reliable for safely checking whether a suspicious URL is malicious?
options: Opening the URL in a private or incognito browser window to limit tracking
Scanning the URL with VirusTotal or Google Safe Browsing before visiting it

questions.q: True or False: Most apps genuinely need always-on location access to function correctly.
options: True — always-on access is required for apps to deliver timely location-based features
False — almost no apps need always-on location; 'While Using' is sufficient for the vast majority

questions.q: How did Cambridge Analytica harvest data from 87 million Facebook users without most of them consenting?
options: They hacked Facebook's database directly using a SQL injection vulnerability
A personality quiz app exploited Facebook's API to collect not just the quiz-taker's data but also all of their friends' data

questions.q: What is the principle of data minimization in the context of privacy?
options: Compressing personal data files so they take up less storage space on servers
Sharing only the minimum amount of personal information necessary, because every piece shared becomes a potential breach exposure

questions.q: True or False: Data brokers compile profiles including addresses, phone numbers, and relatives' names that can be used for targeted spear phishing.
options: True
False — data brokers only sell anonymized aggregate statistics, not individual profiles

questions.q: Why is sharing your real phone number for SMS 2FA a potential privacy and security risk?
options: Phone numbers can be used for SIM swapping attacks, where attackers transfer your number to steal SMS codes
Phone numbers allow apps to track your location without needing location permission

questions.q: According to the NIST SP 800-61 incident response framework, what should be your first action after discovering a compromise?
options: Containment — stop the attacker's active access before investigating the full scope
Root cause analysis — understand how the attacker got in before taking any other action

questions.q: True or False: Changing your password after an account compromise is sufficient to fully evict an attacker.
options: True — a password change immediately terminates all attacker access
False — attackers can maintain access through OAuth app tokens and email forwarding rules that survive password resets

questions.q: What was the entry point for the Colonial Pipeline ransomware attack?
options: A zero-day vulnerability in the pipeline's industrial control system software
A single compromised VPN password with no 2FA enabled on the account

questions.q: True or False: During incident response, documenting the timeline and evidence should wait until after full recovery is complete.

questions.q: True or False: If a cloud workload is within the authorized IP range, no additional cloud provider approval is needed before testing it.

False — cloud workloads require explicit cloud provider authorization; AWS, Azure, and GCP each have their own pentest approval policies

questions.q: A penetration tester scans 192.168.30.0/24, which is the core banking network listed as explicitly out of scope. What must happen next?
options: The tester should document the out-of-scope access and disclose it to the client within 24 hours, then cease all testing immediately
The tester should continue if the scan was passive and did not include any active exploitation attempts

questions.q: The AT&T/iPad incident in 2010 illustrated which principle about security research authorization?
options: Defensive intent and responsible disclosure protect researchers from prosecution under the CFAA regardless of written authorization
Without written authorization from the system owner, access to a system — even with defensive intent — can be prosecuted as unauthorized access

questions.q: True or False: A scope document that lists authorized IP ranges but does not explicitly list out-of-scope systems provides adequate protection for the tester.
options: True — anything not in the authorized range is implicitly out of scope
False — out-of-scope systems must be explicitly listed; ambiguity in scope documents protects neither the client nor the tester

questions.q: ISACA defines three attributes that make audit evidence sufficient. Which combination is correct?
options: Sufficient, reliable, and relevant — evidence must be enough to support the conclusion, from a credible source, and must actually address the control tested
Complete, objective, and documented — evidence must cover the full population, be free of bias, and exist in written form

questions.q: True or False: In attribute sampling, if the sample deviation rate exceeds the tolerable deviation rate, the auditor must conclude the control is not operating effectively.
options: True — a sample deviation rate above the tolerable threshold means the auditor must conclude the control is not operating effectively or expand testing
False — a single sample result is not sufficient to draw any conclusion about control effectiveness

questions.q: In a sample of 60 change tickets, 5 have no CAB approval. The tolerable deviation rate is 5%. What is the sample deviation rate and what does the auditor conclude?
options: 8.3% — this exceeds the 5% tolerable rate, so the change management control is not operating effectively
8.3% — this result is within the acceptable confidence interval since the population is 1,450 tickets

questions.q: The Arthur Andersen case established which principle about audit evidence?
options: Digital evidence must be collected using automated tools to ensure objectivity and completeness
Audit evidence must be preserved, not just collected — destruction of evidence is obstruction of justice, and retention of 7 years is required

questions.q: True or False: Testimonial evidence (interviews) is ranked higher in audit evidence quality than documentary evidence (records and logs).
options: True — direct interviews with process owners provide the most reliable evidence about control operation
False — documentary evidence ranks higher than testimonial evidence; physical evidence observed directly ranks highest of all

questions.q: The CISA standard for a complete audit finding requires four elements. Which set is correct?
options: Condition, Criteria, Cause, and Effect — what was found, what the standard requires, why the deviation exists, and what risk it creates
Finding, Evidence, Impact, and Recommendation — the observation, supporting data, business consequence, and suggested fix

questions.q: True or False: When a finding identified in a prior audit cycle appears unchanged in the current audit, the auditor should report it to management and allow them a second remediation period before escalating.
options: True — management should be given an additional remediation opportunity before the audit committee is involved
False — repeat findings are escalated directly to the audit committee because management accountability has demonstrably failed

questions.q: Remediation tracking at 30, 60, and 90 days after an audit report is issued is best described as:
options: An optional best practice that demonstrates audit quality but is not required by ISACA standards
An audit control itself — untracked remediation is indistinguishable from no remediation

questions.q: The Wells Fargo case (2016–2023) is used in CISA curriculum primarily to illustrate what audit risk?
options: The risk that external auditors will fail to detect fraud when management uses complex financial instruments to conceal it
The risk that management acknowledges findings and agrees to remediation plans but fails to implement them — making repeat findings a fundamental control environment failure

questions.q: True or False: Finding F-2026-01 (terminated user accounts not deprovisioned) is a repeat finding if F-2025-01 from the prior year audit described the same condition and was never remediated.
options: True — same condition, same control, not remediated in 12+ months makes this a repeat finding requiring audit committee escalation
False — the finding IDs are different, so each year's instance is treated as a new finding with a fresh remediation timeline

questions.q: BOLA is ranked #1 in the OWASP API Security Top 10 because an API that checks a valid token but does not verify object ownership allows any authenticated user to access any other user's data.
options: True
False

questions.q: Which technique is the primary method for testing BOLA in an API audit?
options: Swapping object IDs between authenticated test accounts
Sending malformed JSON payloads to crash the server

questions.q: In the Peloton API breach (2021), what made the BOLA vulnerability especially severe?
options: Some endpoints required no authentication at all
Peloton stored passwords in plaintext in its database

questions.q: When auditing an API for BOLA, it is sufficient to test only the documented, publicly listed endpoints.
options: False — undocumented endpoints must also be enumerated
True — undocumented endpoints are not reachable by attackers

questions.q: A secure API response to a BOLA test attempt (User A accessing User B's object) should be:
options: HTTP 403 Forbidden with no object data returned
HTTP 200 OK with an empty response body

questions.q: Running 'git rm' on a file containing a secret fully removes the secret from the repository because no further copies exist.
options: False — the secret remains in git history on every clone and fork
True — git rm deletes the file from all branches and history

questions.q: Which HashiCorp Vault feature eliminates the risk of a stolen static database credential being used by an attacker?
options: Dynamic secrets with short TTLs generated per request
Storing secrets in an encrypted YAML file checked into git

questions.q: In the 2022 Uber breach, what was the root cause that enabled the attacker's lateral movement?
options: A hardcoded admin credential in a PowerShell script on an internal file share
An unpatched vulnerability in Uber's public-facing web application

questions.q: Secrets scanning programs must cover source code repositories, CI/CD pipeline variables, container images, file shares, and internal scripts.
options: True
False — CI/CD variables are automatically encrypted by the pipeline provider

questions.q: What is the recommended maximum secret TTL for automated systems accessing HashiCorp Vault?
options: 1 hour
24 hours

questions.q: AWS Service Control Policies (SCPs) can be overridden by an IAM administrator within the same account if their IAM policy explicitly grants the action.
options: False — SCPs override IAM policies and cannot be bypassed by in-account admins
True — IAM AdministratorAccess always takes precedence over SCPs

questions.q: In the Capital One breach (2019), which two guardrail failures allowed the exfiltration of 106 million records?
options: No Config rule caught the WAF misconfiguration and no CloudWatch alert triggered on S3 call volume
CloudTrail was disabled and the S3 bucket had public access enabled

questions.q: CloudTrail must be enabled in all AWS regions, not just us-east-1, to ensure full API activity logging.
options: True
False — us-east-1 captures global services so other regions are optional

questions.q: Which AWS control provides CONTINUOUS compliance monitoring of resources against defined policies?
options: AWS Config Rules
AWS CloudTrail

questions.q: Storing CloudTrail logs in the same account being audited is a security best practice because it simplifies log access.
options: False — a compromised admin in the same account could delete logs; cross-account storage is required
True — same-account storage ensures the audit team has fast access

questions.q: The IAM permission 'iam:CreatePolicyVersion' alone can represent a privilege escalation path to AdministratorAccess.
options: True — a user can create a new policy version granting full admin and attach it to themselves
False — CreatePolicyVersion only modifies existing policies without changing effective permissions

questions.q: In the 2022 Okta breach, what IAM principle had been violated that directly enabled the attacker's access?
options: A third-party vendor support account had broader permissions than needed for its function
Okta had failed to enable MFA on its root AWS account

questions.q: Which combination of IAM permissions creates a privilege escalation path by allowing an attacker to launch EC2 instances with privileged roles attached?
options: iam:PassRole combined with ec2:RunInstances
iam:ListRoles combined with ec2:DescribeInstances

questions.q: Access keys older than 90 days on active IAM users are considered a security finding.
options: True
False — key age is irrelevant if the key has not been used recently

questions.q: For applications, AWS IAM best practice recommends using IAM roles rather than IAM users with long-lived access keys.
options: True — roles use temporary credentials with no access keys to rotate
False — IAM users are preferred because they have a fixed identity for audit logs

questions.q: Mounting the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) inside a container effectively grants that container full control over the host.
options: True
False — the socket only allows the container to view other containers, not control them

questions.q: In the 2018 Tesla Kubernetes cryptojacking incident, what initial access point did the attackers exploit?
options: A Kubernetes dashboard exposed to the internet without authentication
A container running as root that allowed privilege escalation

questions.q: Kubernetes etcd stores Secrets encrypted by default, so additional encryption configuration is not required.
options: False — Kubernetes Secrets are base64 encoded (not encrypted) by default; encryption at rest must be explicitly configured
True — etcd uses AES-256 encryption for all stored objects automatically

questions.q: Container image CVE scanning should occur in the CI/CD pipeline before images are pushed to the registry.
options: True — catching CVEs pre-registry prevents vulnerable images from ever reaching production
False — scanning after deployment is sufficient because runtime security controls compensate

questions.q: A container specification with 'runAsUser: 0' and 'privileged: true' represents which risk in a Kubernetes audit?
options: The container runs as root with full host kernel capabilities — a process escape equals root on the host node
The container has read access to the host filesystem but cannot execute processes outside its namespace

questions.q: IaC security scanning in CI/CD catches misconfigurations before infrastructure is deployed, shifting security left.
options: True
False — IaC scanning can only run after deployment to check actual resource state

questions.q: In the Toyota Connected Services breach (2023), what single automated control would have caught the misconfiguration on day one in 2013?
options: The AWS Config rule 's3-bucket-public-read-prohibited' running continuously
A manual penetration test scheduled quarterly

questions.q: A Terraform security group resource allowing ingress from '0.0.0.0/0' on port 22 is classified as what severity by tfsec?
options: HIGH — SSH open to the entire internet is an automatic significant finding
LOW — port 22 is a standard administrative port and expected to be accessible

questions.q: Which tool performs static security analysis specifically on Terraform templates?
options: tfsec or Checkov
Trivy or Grype

questions.q: Legacy cloud resources that predate IaC management do not need to be imported into IaC scanning because historical configurations are exempt from current standards.
options: False — legacy resources must be imported into IaC management and subjected to the same scanning as new deployments
True — retroactive IaC scanning is not feasible and creates unnecessary operational overhead

questions.q: SAST (Static Application Security Testing) analyzes source code without executing it, finding vulnerabilities like SQL injection before the application is built.
options: True
False — SAST requires a running application to simulate attacks against live endpoints

questions.q: The Equifax breach (2017) was enabled because the company did not have SCA (Software Composition Analysis) integrated into its CI/CD pipeline to detect the vulnerable Apache Struts dependency.
options: True — SCA would have flagged the CVE immediately when the patch was released
False — Equifax had SCA but chose to suppress the finding manually

questions.q: Who should authorize false positive suppressions in a SAST program?
options: A security engineer with documented justification
The developer who wrote the flagged code, since they best understand the context

questions.q: Which type of security testing confirms that a vulnerability found by SAST is actually exploitable in the running application?
options: DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing)
SCA (Software Composition Analysis)

questions.q: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) is an example of a vulnerability that SCA tools can detect because it exists in a known open-source dependency.
options: True
False — Log4Shell is a runtime behavior and cannot be detected by static dependency scanning

questions.q: The 2013 Target breach succeeded because an HVAC vendor's network access was on the same flat network as Target's point-of-sale systems.
options: True
False — the attacker pivoted through a web application vulnerability, not a flat network

questions.q: In a properly segmented AWS environment, an RDS database in a private subnet should have a security group that allows port 5432 from which source?
options: Only the application server security group ID
0.0.0.0/0, to ensure the application can always reach the database

questions.q: VPC Flow Logs must be enabled to validate network segmentation because they provide the only record of actual traffic between subnets.
options: True
False — security group rules are sufficient proof that segmentation is working without needing traffic logs

questions.q: PCI DSS requires network segmentation to reduce the scope of the cardholder data environment and limit lateral movement.
options: True
False — PCI DSS recommends segmentation but does not require it for compliance

questions.q: A database subnet with a NAT gateway route that allows outbound internet access represents what type of segmentation finding?
options: A segmentation gap — database servers should have no outbound internet route
An acceptable configuration — NAT allows outbound only and does not expose the database to inbound connections

questions.q: Database audit logs that capture only DDL and DML operations (CREATE, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) are insufficient because they miss read-based exfiltration via SELECT queries.
options: True
False — SELECT queries do not modify data so they present no compliance logging requirement

questions.q: In the Morgan Stanley decommission breach (2020), what single database control would have rendered the data on the decommissioned hardware unreadable?
options: Encryption at rest — data without the encryption key is unreadable even on recovered hardware
Column-level masking — masked fields would have appeared as placeholder values

questions.q: A Snowflake service account (SVC_ETL) granted the ACCOUNTADMIN role when it only needs INSERT access to staging tables is classified as which type of finding?
options: Critical — the account has superadmin rights, meaning any compromise grants full database access
Medium — ACCOUNTADMIN is standard for service accounts that run scheduled ETL jobs

questions.q: Row-level security (RLS) limits the data a compromised user account can access by partitioning rows based on the authenticated user's attributes.
options: True
False — RLS only applies to database administrators and does not affect regular analyst accounts

questions.q: A development database containing copies of production PII must have encryption at rest enabled, even though it is not the production environment.
options: True — any database containing sensitive data requires encryption regardless of environment label
False — development databases are excluded from encryption requirements because they are not customer-facing

questions.q: In the Equifax breach (2017), attackers went undetected for 78 days primarily because an SSL inspection appliance had an expired certificate and was not inspecting or logging HTTPS traffic.
options: True
False — the attacker used encrypted tunnels that no monitoring system could detect at the time

questions.q: How is a SIEM log coverage gap identified during an audit?
options: By comparing the SIEM log source list against the full asset inventory
By reviewing the SIEM alert backlog for systems that have never generated an alert

questions.q: PCI DSS Requirement 10 mandates a minimum log retention of 12 months, with at least 3 months immediately accessible.
options: True
False — PCI DSS requires 6 months of retention with 1 month immediately accessible

questions.q: A SIEM with 4,237 unacknowledged alerts and an MTTA of 18.4 hours indicates an effective monitoring program.
options: False — this represents alert fatigue; effective monitoring requires MTTA under 15 minutes for critical alerts
True — a large alert backlog demonstrates comprehensive detection coverage

questions.q: Verifying that log sources are actively SENDING logs — not just configured to do so — is a required step in a log coverage audit.
options: True
False — configuration verification is sufficient; active log delivery can be assumed if no errors are reported

questions.q: Zero Trust architecture operates on the principle that users and devices inside the corporate network should be trusted by default because they have already passed perimeter controls.
options: False — Zero Trust requires every access request to be authenticated and authorized regardless of network location
True — internal network position is a valid trust signal in a Zero Trust model

questions.q: The SolarWinds attack (2020) demonstrated a core weakness of perimeter-based security by exploiting which condition?
options: Implicit internal trust that allowed the attacker to move laterally once inside the perimeter
A Zero Trust misconfiguration that exposed internal APIs to the internet

questions.q: Which authentication method represents the ADVANCED maturity level for the Identity pillar in CISA's Zero Trust Maturity Model?
options: Phishing-resistant FIDO2 hardware keys replacing SMS or TOTP
Username and password with optional SMS one-time codes

questions.q: ZTNA (Zero Trust Network Access) replaces VPN by providing application-specific access without granting broad network-level access.
options: True
False — ZTNA and VPN are complementary and both are required in a mature Zero Trust architecture

questions.q: Google's BeyondCorp (2014) was significant because it was the first major enterprise implementation demonstrating that internal network location should provide no additional trust.
options: True
False — BeyondCorp still maintained a trusted internal zone for engineering workstations

questions.q: Manual compliance audits provide continuous, real-time coverage of cloud resources, whereas automated compliance checks only capture a point-in-time snapshot.
options: False — automated compliance is continuous; manual audits are point-in-time snapshots
True — human auditors can assess context that automated tools miss, making them the primary control

questions.q: Toyota's 10-year S3 public access misconfiguration (2013–2023) would have been caught immediately by which AWS service?
options: AWS Config rule 's3-bucket-public-read-prohibited' running continuously
AWS CloudTrail logging all S3 API activity

questions.q: AWS Security Hub provides a unified compliance score by aggregating findings from Config Rules, GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and third-party tools.
options: True
False — Security Hub only aggregates GuardDuty findings and does not integrate with Config Rules

questions.q: Auto-remediation of a Config Rule finding (such as disabling a public S3 bucket) should be logged to provide an audit trail.
options: True
False — auto-remediation actions are deterministic and do not need to be logged

questions.q: A compliance automation program where only 3 of 67 Config rules have auto-remediation enabled and findings sit an average of 47 days before remediation represents a mature compliance posture.
options: False — effective compliance automation requires auto-remediation enabled for critical and high rules with prompt remediation
True — 47 days is within the acceptable remediation window for most compliance frameworks

questions.q: In Claude's tool use API, what does Claude use to decide when to call a specific tool?
options: The tool's description field
The tool's input_schema field

questions.q: True or False: In the agentic loop, Claude executes tools directly inside the API call.
options: True — Claude runs tools internally
False — your code executes the tool and returns a tool_result

questions.q: Which Capital One breach vector would an agentic audit tool polling WAF rules have caught early?
options: A deviation from the known-good WAF configuration baseline
An unencrypted S3 bucket created by a developer

questions.q: A tool definition requires three parts. Which set is correct?
options: name, description, and input_schema
name, endpoint, and output_schema

questions.q: True or False: You should always validate tool inputs server-side because Claude always passes perfectly shaped arguments.
options: True — Claude never sends unexpected argument shapes
False — Claude can pass unexpected argument shapes; always validate server-side

questions.q: What percentage of organizations typically have undocumented API endpoints not in their official inventory?
options: 60% or more
Less than 10%

questions.q: True or False: In agentic API enumeration, Claude should enforce rate limits and scope rules directly through its prompt reasoning.
options: True — Claude's judgment is sufficient for scope enforcement
False — rate limits and scope must be enforced in tool implementations, not by Claude

questions.q: The Peloton API exposure (2021) was discovered because one API version required authentication while another did not. What does this illustrate?
options: Auth regression — a newer version weakened authentication from a previous version
An overly permissive IAM role attached to the API server

questions.q: In the enumeration agent loop, what does Claude do when stop_reason is 'end_turn'?
options: It has synthesized its final report and no more tool calls are needed
It encountered an error and the loop must restart from the beginning

questions.q: True or False: The primary technique for finding shadow APIs is comparing the live API gateway route table against the official OpenAPI spec.
options: True — diffing live routes vs documented spec reveals undocumented endpoints
False — shadow APIs are found by brute-forcing all possible URL paths

questions.q: What is the main limitation of regex-only secrets scanners like truffleHog?
options: They catch well-formatted secrets but miss obfuscated credentials and context-dependent cases
They are too slow for production use on large repositories

questions.q: True or False: Deleting a file containing a secret from a git repository permanently removes the secret from all copies of the repo.
options: True — git rm removes the file from history
False — the secret remains in git history; every developer who cloned the repo still has it

questions.q: In the two-phase scanning architecture, what is Claude's classification output for each finding?
options: confirmed_secret, likely_placeholder, or false_positive
critical, high, medium, or low

questions.q: The 2022 Uber breach involved credentials in PowerShell scripts on a network share. What does this highlight?
options: Secrets scanning must extend beyond git repos to CI configs, IaC, operational scripts, and more
Regex scanners are sufficient when they cover all git branches including feature branches

questions.q: True or False: AI-augmented secret classification can reduce analyst review time by 80% or more compared to regex-only output.
options: True — Claude's context-aware classification dramatically reduces false positive noise
False — AI classification adds processing overhead that slows triage

questions.q: Why is cloud resource inventory considered a prerequisite to all subsequent audit checks?
options: You cannot assess controls on resources you have not enumerated
Cloud providers require inventory submission before granting audit API access

questions.q: True or False: Toyota's 2023 data exposure lasted 10 years because the misconfiguration was technically undetectable.
options: True — the public access setting was a hidden cloud provider feature
False — it was a simple public-access misconfiguration that automated weekly scanning would have caught

questions.q: What does a resource dependency graph reveal that a standard inventory list does not?
options: Which IAM roles can access which buckets, enabling privilege chain analysis
The geographic region where each resource is deployed

questions.q: Which finding is considered CRITICAL in a cloud enumeration audit?
options: A Lambda function with AdministratorAccess attached
An S3 bucket without versioning enabled

questions.q: True or False: A cloud enumeration agent using the AWS SDK can complete an inventory in minutes, compared to days for manual review.
options: True — agentic enumeration is dramatically faster than manual console review
False — API rate limits make agentic enumeration slower than manual review

questions.q: Which IAM action is a classic privilege escalation vector that Claude's policy analyzer specifically looks for?
options: iam:PassRole without Condition constraints
s3:GetObject on a specific bucket ARN

questions.q: True or False: Claude can only detect privilege escalation in individual IAM policy statements, not across multiple policy layers.
options: True — Claude analyzes one statement at a time
False — Claude reasons about effective permissions across identity-based and resource-based policy layers

questions.q: In the Capital One breach, what turned a WAF SSRF vulnerability into a 106-million-record breach?
options: An overly permissive IAM role on the WAF's EC2 instance with broad S3 access
A publicly accessible RDS database without encryption

questions.q: What is the recommended remediation for a policy with s3:* on Resource '*'?
options: Scope the action to specific required operations and the specific bucket ARN
Add a Deny statement for s3:DeleteObject to limit the damage

questions.q: True or False: iam:CreatePolicyVersion is a privilege escalation vector because it lets an attacker create a new version of any policy, including policies attached to admin roles.
options: True — this action can be used to grant admin permissions through an existing policy
False — CreatePolicyVersion only affects policies the caller already owns

questions.q: What is the primary advantage of using MCP (Model Context Protocol) over writing custom tool integrations for each audit system?
options: MCP provides a standardized protocol so pre-built servers work across different Claude environments
MCP gives Claude direct database write access without API keys

questions.q: True or False: The same MCP server configuration works both in Claude Code IDE and in production API pipelines.
options: True — MCP is designed to work uniformly across Claude Code and the Anthropic API
False — MCP servers must be separately configured for IDE versus API use

questions.q: Which transport mechanisms does MCP support?
options: stdio or HTTP+SSE
WebSockets only

questions.q: The SolarWinds SUNBURST attack (2020) succeeded partly because no tooling correlated anomalies across which three systems?
options: Git server, build system, and artifact repository
Firewall logs, DNS records, and IAM audit trails

questions.q: True or False: MCP servers expose two types of primitives: resources (read-only data) and tools (callable functions that can modify state).
options: True — resources and tools are the two core MCP primitives
False — MCP only supports read-only resources; write operations require custom tool integrations

questions.q: At what stage does agentic IaC review integrate into the development workflow?
options: During pull request review, before the infrastructure change is merged and applied
After deployment, by comparing live configuration against the committed Terraform state

questions.q: True or False: Claude must be explicitly programmed with rules like 'publicly_accessible = true on RDS is a finding' to detect IaC misconfigurations.
options: True — Claude requires an exhaustive ruleset to identify each misconfiguration type
False — Claude understands IaC semantics and can identify misconfigurations without explicit rules

questions.q: Which security standard is referenced as the baseline for Terraform audit reviews?
options: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark
GDPR Article 32 technical measures

questions.q: What is the enforcement mechanism that prevents developers from bypassing a critical IaC finding?
options: Critical findings block PR merge via required status checks
Developers receive an email notification and must self-certify the finding is acceptable

questions.q: True or False: The Twitch source code leak (2021) demonstrated that exposed IaC files can help attackers map cloud infrastructure even when not directly breached through IaC.
options: True — Terraform configs in the leak revealed Twitch's AWS architecture to attackers
False — Terraform files contain no sensitive information about live infrastructure

questions.q: What percentage of traditional audit time is typically spent on evidence collection?
options: Approximately 60%
Approximately 10%

questions.q: True or False: Claude's value in evidence validation is confirming that an evidence file exists, not assessing whether it actually proves the control works.
options: True — existence of the evidence file is sufficient for compliance
False — Claude assesses whether the evidence actually satisfies the control requirement, not just its presence

questions.q: What are Claude's three evidence assessment outcomes for each control?
options: satisfied, partially satisfied, or not satisfied
pass, warn, or fail

questions.q: In the Equifax breach (2017), what gap in evidence collection allowed the Apache Struts vulnerability to go undetected?
options: Patch evidence existed but did not verify the specific vulnerable web-facing systems
Equifax had no patch management policy and therefore collected no evidence at all

questions.q: True or False: An agentic evidence collection system maps each artifact to a specific control requirement (e.g., NIST, SOC 2 criterion) and includes Claude's assessment.
options: True — structured mapping to controls with assessment is the key output
False — agentic systems collect raw exports without mapping to specific controls

questions.q: In a multi-agent audit pipeline, what is the orchestrator agent's primary responsibility?
options: Planning the audit, delegating to specialists, synthesizing cross-domain findings, and producing the final report
Running all domain-specific tool calls directly without involving specialist agents

questions.q: True or False: In the orchestrator-specialist pattern, specialist agents should communicate findings directly with each other to improve cross-domain analysis.
options: True — direct specialist communication improves correlation accuracy
False — specialists should not communicate with each other; all synthesis happens at the orchestrator level

questions.q: Why should specialist agents return structured JSON rather than natural language?
options: So the orchestrator can reliably parse and correlate findings across domains
Because Claude cannot read natural language output from subagents

questions.q: The MOVEit breach (2023) involved SQL injection, internet exposure, and database permissions. What does this illustrate about single-domain audits?
options: Each specialist would have partially caught it, but cross-domain synthesis was needed to see the full risk chain
Only a network audit was needed because the root cause was the internet-exposed MOVEit server

questions.q: True or False: Running specialist agents in parallel mirrors how human audit teams with domain specialists operate.
options: True — parallel specialist workstreams match the structure of human audit engagements
False — human audit teams run all workstreams sequentially to avoid conflicting findings

questions.q: What percentage of an auditor's time is typically spent on report writing?
options: 30 to 40 percent
Less than 5 percent

questions.q: True or False: Vague audit recommendations like 'improve IoT security posture' are just as likely to be acted on as specific, owner-assigned, deadline-dated recommendations.
options: True — all audit recommendations receive equal remediation priority
False — vague recommendations get deprioritized; specific recommendations with owners and deadlines get implemented

questions.q: What is the recommended Claude report generation approach for very large audits with 500 or more findings?
options: Chunk by domain to generate section drafts, then synthesize an executive summary from the section summaries
Submit all findings in a single prompt regardless of size to maintain full context

questions.q: Which four elements must every finding section include according to the report generation system prompt pattern?
options: Finding title, observation, risk, and recommendation (plus a management response field)
CVE number, CVSS score, affected asset, and patch deadline

questions.q: True or False: The Dyn DDoS (2016) demonstrated that generic audit report language can delay remediation by failing to produce actionable, prioritized recommendations.
options: True — previous IoT security assessments existed but their vague recommendations were not acted on
False — Dyn had no prior security assessments covering IoT risks

questions.q: What is the fundamental limitation of traditional point-in-time IT audits compared to continuous compliance monitoring?
options: Point-in-time audits capture control state at the moment of audit, which may be months or years stale by the time it matters
Point-in-time audits are more expensive than continuous monitoring

questions.q: True or False: When a continuous monitoring sentinel detects a CRITICAL finding, it should only log the finding for the next scheduled audit review.
options: True — logging is the appropriate response for automated sentinel findings
False — CRITICAL findings should immediately trigger PagerDuty, Slack alerts, and a Jira ticket

questions.q: Which scheduling mechanism is described for running a Claude compliance agent on a daily cron in AWS?
options: AWS EventBridge rule triggering a Lambda function
AWS CloudWatch alarm polling an SNS topic

questions.q: The LastPass breach (2022) had a four-month dwell time. What monitoring capability would have shortened the attacker's window?
options: Behavioral baseline monitoring detecting anomalous IAM access patterns and authentication events
Daily static configuration snapshots of S3 bucket permissions

questions.q: True or False: Suppression rules in a continuous monitoring system are important to prevent alert fatigue for known-accepted risks with compensating controls.
options: True — without suppression, recurring known-acceptable findings cause analysts to ignore real alerts
False — all findings should always generate alerts regardless of known acceptance status

questions.q: In the full agentic audit pipeline, which phase must complete sequentially before the parallel domain analysis phases can begin?
options: Phase 1 inventory enumeration, because all domain agents depend on the resource list
Phase 3 synthesis, because domain agents need the cross-domain context first

questions.q: True or False: The agentic audit pipeline is intended to replace auditors entirely by automating all audit judgment.
options: True — the goal is fully autonomous auditing without human involvement
False — agents eliminate mechanical work so auditors can focus on judgment, risk context, and stakeholder communication

questions.q: Which two output phases of the full pipeline can run in parallel after synthesis completes?
